summary: Large fighter cleanup/decomp pass that removed asm/melee/ft/ftparts.s from the build and replaced it with src/melee/ft/ftparts.c containing mostly inline-asm C wrappers, class-info globals, data/bss/sdata labels, and named fighter JObj/PObj entry points. The PR also propagated symbol cleanup around matrix inverse and fighter JObj rotation, renaming func_80379310 to HSD_MtxInverse and func_8007584C to Fighter_JObjSetRotation in callsites/headers/asm labels. It adjusted baselib headers for JObj scale accessors and PObjInfo layout, updated obj_files.mk to build ftparts.c, and made small fighter.c comment/callsite cleanups. PR body notes it included equivalent commits from #636 through commit 96e5f44, with some squashed, and that a later source-branch commit range could not be made to match.
what_changed: Deleted the 3315-line asm/melee/ft/ftparts.s and switched obj_files.mk from asm/melee/ft/ftparts.s.o to src/melee/ft/ftparts.c.o.
Expanded src/melee/ft/ftparts.c from a small stub into a large C translation unit with includes for fighter, ftanim, ftdata, lbrefract, GX/MTX, and sysdolphin baselib headers.
Added ftparts.c globals and labels including ftJObj, ftIntpJObj, ftPObj, fighter_class_library, ft_intp_jobj, ftparts.c assertion strings, lbl_80459240, lbl_804590D8, and sdata/sdata2 constants.

Introduced named class/init/setup routines in ftparts.c such as Fighter_JObjInfoInit, Fighter_IntpJObjInfoInit, Fighter_PObjInfoInit, Fighter_JObjMakePositionMtx, Fighter_IntpJObjLoad, Fighter_PObjSetupMtx, and Fighter_JObjSetRotation, while many remaining ftparts functions stayed as asm functions with address-like names.

Renamed matrix inverse symbol func_80379310 to HSD_MtxInverse in include/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.h, src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.c, asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s, fighter.c, ftanim.s, lbcollision.s, and within ftparts-related callsites.
Renamed fighter rotation setter callsites from func_8007584C to Fighter_JObjSetRotation in ft_unknown_006.s and fighter.c, and declared Fighter_JObjSetRotation in ftparts.h.
Added inline HSD_JObjGetScaleX, HSD_JObjGetScaleY, and HSD_JObjGetScaleZ helpers to include/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h with assert_line checks.
Removed the update function pointer from HSD_PObjInfo in include/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.h.
Added src/melee/ft/ftparts.h.orig with a set of ftparts prototypes, including both named and still-unknown func_8007xxxx symbols.
Made small cleanup/comment changes in src/melee/ft/fighter.c around effective friction comments and replacement of raw function names with named helpers.
smart_moves: Moved ftparts into the C build even though many functions were still inline asm, enabling incremental decomp progress without requiring a fully high-level rewrite in one PR.
Used symbol renaming across C headers, source, and asm labels so already-identified library/fighter routines became searchable and reusable by later decomp work.
Preserved exact matching constraints by keeping difficult ftparts bodies as asm functions in src/melee/ft/ftparts.c while still attaching meaningful prototypes and class-info setup around them.
Centralized ftparts-related includes and externs in the C translation unit, which made dependencies on JObj, PObj, MObj, perf stats, matrix routines, and fighter data explicit.
Updated obj_files.mk in tandem with deleting the asm file, avoiding duplicate object inclusion and ensuring the new C translation unit owned the ftparts text range.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter modules, an asm-to-C migration can be valuable even when most functions remain inline asm: it unlocks named globals, headers, prototypes, and gradual high-level replacement.
When a raw function name is confidently identified, rename it everywhere at once: callsites in asm, declarations in headers, and source references should converge on the semantic name.
Class-info initialization functions are useful anchors for decomp: ftJObj, ftIntpJObj, and ftPObj made the JObj/PObj virtual method relationships explicit even before all methods were high-level C.
Matrix-related routines should use baselib naming once identified; HSD_MtxInverse is more reusable and less ambiguous than func_80379310.

PR body evidence matters for matching status: this pass explicitly included equivalent commits from #636 through a known commit, but also documented that one source-branch range could not be made to match.

Header layout corrections can be matching-critical; this PR removed HSD_PObjInfo.update and added JObj scale inline helpers, changing how later code can express field accesses and vtable/class layouts.
Keep assertion strings and original data-section labels when migrating from asm to C; they document source-file identity, line/assert context, and data ordering needed for matching.
naming_conventions: func_80379310 was renamed to HSD_MtxInverse, following the sysdolphin/baselib HSD_Mtx* naming convention.
func_8007584C was renamed to Fighter_JObjSetRotation, using the project’s Fighter_* prefix for fighter-specific helpers operating on HSD_JObj.
Fighter_JObjInfoInit, Fighter_IntpJObjInfoInit, and Fighter_PObjInfoInit use explicit Fighter_* class initialization names instead of anonymous func_8007xxxx symbols.
New baselib JObj accessors follow HSD_JObjGetScaleX/Y/Z naming and inline assert_line style.
Unidentified functions in ftparts.c remained as func_8007xxxx names, preserving address-based names until stronger evidence exists.
Data labels such as lbl_803C09E0 and lbl_804D3A40 were retained where semantic names were not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted a standalone asm file and recreated its contents inside a C translation unit using asm function bodies, nofralloc, preserved labels, and original instruction comments.
Used #pragma push and clang-format-off style around inline asm blocks to preserve assembler-sensitive formatting.
Matched class/vtable-like setup by assigning function pointers such as HSD_JOBJ_INFO(&ftJObj)->make_pmtx = Fighter_JObjMakePositionMtx and HSD_JOBJ_INFO(&ftIntpJObj)->load = Fighter_IntpJObjLoad.

Kept exact asm calls for GX/HSD matrix operations such as PSMTXScale, PSMTXConcat, HSD_MtxInverseTranspose, GXLoadPosMtxImm, GXLoadNrmMtxImm, and GXLoadTexMtxImm while giving enclosing functions better names.
Adjusted asm labels in non-converted files so branch/call comments refer to HSD_MtxInverse or Fighter_JObjSetRotation without changing code bytes.
Documented a known non-matching commit range in the PR body instead of implying the entire source-branch history matched.

review_feedback.note: No line review comments or textual reviewer feedback were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for removal or cleanup of src/melee/ft/ftparts.h.orig, since this PR added it as a .orig file.
Search for subsequent high-level C replacements of asm functions in src/melee/ft/ftparts.c, especially Fighter_JObjMakePositionMtx, Fighter_PObjSetupMtx, and Fighter_JObjSetRotation.
Verify whether HSD_JObjGetScaleZ returning jobj->scale.x was intentional for matching or later corrected.
Look for follow-up changes that resolve the PR body’s non-matching commit range 1c59df00c11ab1123ca0c74db92a27516b13453f..5d43ea4c805c4ffec4e80c2208687f73ccfca5fb.
Audit later uses of HSD_PObjInfo after the update function pointer was removed to understand the finalized baselib PObj layout.
Search for PRs that continue symbol cleanup from func_80074xxx ftparts names to semantic Fighter_* names.
